channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Ben Simmons waived by the Nets after the team and former No. 1 pick agree to a buyout
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ben Simmons was waived by the Brooklyn Nets on Saturday after the team agreed to a buyout with the last remaining link to a failed era of building around All-Stars. The No.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Utah HC gives up season-high 7 goals to Carolina
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (ABC4 Sports) - After two overtime thrilling victories, the Utah Hockey Club gave up a season-high seven goals in a 7-3 loss Saturday to the Carolina Hurricanes. Seth Jarvis had two goals and two assists, and Sebastian Aho had two goals and an assist for the Hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Emiliano Grillo's hole-in-one at famed 16th hole at Phoenix Open sparks massive fan celebration
item.description: Emiliano Grillo’s hole-in-one in the second round Friday at the Waste Management Open gave the fans at the rowdiest hole in golf a reason to go wild.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Anthony Davis leaves his Dallas debut with an injury after seismic Luka Doncic trade
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Anthony Davis has left the bench area in his debut with the Dallas Mavericks after sustaining an injury late in the third quarter Saturday against Houston. The team said he was doubtful to return because of a lower-body injury.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ahead of Real Madrid clash, Man City given a scare by third-tier Leyton Orient in the FA Cup
item.description: Ahead of a Champions League playoff against Real Madrid, Manchester City survived a scare at third-tier Leyton Orient to advance to the fifth round of the FA Cup. City hosts Madrid on Tuesday in the first of a two-legged playoff.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ROBSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Eagles defensive end Brandon Graham returns from torn triceps for Super Bowl
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Philadelphia Eagles star defensive end Brandon Graham was cleared to return from a torn triceps suffered 11 weeks ago and will play in the Super Bowl against Kansas City. The 36-year-old Graham was thought to be done for the season after he was injured in a Nov.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: California residents protest, threaten lawsuits over state's refusal to follow Trump's trans athletes ban
item.description: California residents gathered outside a public school federation board meeting to protest its intent to not follow Trump's order on banning trans athletes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former NFL player, coach Dick Jauron dead at 74
item.description: Dick Jauron, who played defensive back in the NFL before moving into coaching, died one day before one his former teams competes in Super Bowl LIX.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NFL stars team up to surprise Make-A-Wish group at LIDS ahead of Super Bowl LIX
item.description: The Super Bowl is all about making memories, and 19 Make-A-Wish kids and their families got that when they were surprised by Jahmyr Gibbs, Tristan Wirfs and Cam Skattebo at LIDS.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CMU Football signing day recap
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)— The football program is entering a new era after 42 student-athletes officially committed to the Mavericks, officials said. “The thing I tell these guys when they go through recruiting is, ‘You’ve got to be where your feet are.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Eagles-Chiefs Super Bowl is a matchup of some of the brightest and oldest minds in the NFL
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Andy Reid and Matt Nagy vs. Vic Fangio. Nick Sirianni and Kellen Moore vs. Steve Spagnuolo. This Super Bowl is a matchup of some of the brightest — and oldest — minds in the NFL. Reid and Fangio are 66. Spagnuolo is 65.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Rookies for Eagles and Chiefs could play a key role in the Super Bowl
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Reed Blankenship was a part-time player as a rookie during Philadelphia's Super Bowl run two years ago and only got on the field for one defensive play against the Kansas City Chiefs.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McCallum wins all-around as Red Rocks beat Arizona
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Grace McCallum won the all-around with a score of 39.65 to lead the 4th-ranked Utah gymnastics team past #19 Arizona, 197.675 to 196.125.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Jake Paul says boxing champ Canelo Álvarez 'ducked' him after 'deep negotiations' for fight
item.description: Jake Paul and Canelo Álvarez were close to coming to terms on a fight in May, but, after it fell through, Paul says the champ is avoiding him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Anthony Davis is set for his Mavs debut and understands the ire of fans who lost Luka Doncic
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Anthony Davis expects to make his debut for the Dallas Mavericks in their first home game since the seismic trade that sent Luka Doncic to the Los Angeles Lakers.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tiger Woods to play at Torrey Pines, his first PGA Tour event since July
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Tiger Woods committed Friday to playing the Genesis Invitational at Torrey Pines, returning to one of his favorite venues for his first PGA Tour start since the British Open last July.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NFL has no plans to end DEI practices, commissioner says
item.description: The NFL is committed to sticking to its DEI policies despite a change in its Super Bowl on-field messaging campaign
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Negro Leagues Baseball Museum space, message expands with $30 million project
item.description: The shrine to some of baseball's best and previously-forgotten players is getting too big to miss largely out of necessity, and one of baseball's best storytellers talks about how the Negro Leagues Baseball Museum is looking to expand its mission.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Barry, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox officiating analyst Mike Pereira dismisses 'myth' of pro-Chiefs favoritism
item.description: For the past few weeks, the officials have been nearly as big of a story as the players on the field thanks to mostly online conspiracy theories that NFL officials are biased in favor of Patrick Mahomes and the Kansas City Chiefs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Spring training preview: Dodgers are favored to be MLB's first repeat champion in a quarter-century
item.description: Miguel Rojas gladly gave up his No. 11 to Roki Sasaki and went back to 72, his jersey as a Los Angeles Dodgers rookie in 2014. “I'm not asking for anything. I have everything that I want in life,” the 35-year-old infielder said. "All I ask for him is trying to get us another championship.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: SDCC coach battles cancer while coaching
item.description: Jose Martinez's whole life is basketball, from playing on the Puerto Rican national team at 15 years old, to becoming an assistant coach at San Diego City College.
item.dc:creator: Frank Jefferson, Fox 5

item.title: Jason Kelce and look-alike posse take on New Orleans
item.description: Five of the most passionate NFL fan duos in America were challenged to be the first team to find the real Jason Kelce.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: MLB owners vote to install John Seidler as control person of San Diego Padres
item.description: Baseball owners voted Thursday to install John Seidler, the oldest brother of the late former Padres chairman Peter Seidler, as the team’s control person.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Virginia Halas McCaskey, longtime Bears owner and matriarch, dies at 102, team announces
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. — Virginia Halas McCaskey, the longtime owner and matriarch of the Chicago Bears, died Thursday at 102, the team announced in a news release. Virginia served as the owner of the Bears since her father and team founder George S. Halas died on Oct. 31, 1983.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Three UPenn teammates of Lia Thomas sue university
item.description: Three former teammates of transgender swimmer Lia Thomas sued the University of Pennsylvania over rights violations
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: McDavid makes perfect pass in overtime as the Oilers beat the Blackhawks 4-3
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Connor McDavid set up Zach Hyman in overtime, and the Edmonton Oilers beat the Chicago Blackhawks 4-3 on Wednesday night. Hyman redirected McDavid's pass for the power-play goal at 1:36. It was Hyman's 19th goal and McDavid's 49th assist of the season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Edwards scores 49 to lead Wolves over short-handed Bulls 127-108
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Anthony Edwards scored 49 points, including 20 in the third quarter, as the Minnesota Timberwolves beat the short-handed Chicago Bulls 127-108 on Wednesday night. Edwards hit six 3-pointers and was 17 of 18 from the free-throw line and also added nine rebounds.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Warriors to trade for Miami's Jimmy Butler: ESPN
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The Golden State Warriors are finalizing a blockbuster trade for Miami Heat star Jimmy Butler, ESPN reported Wednesday. Butler, 35, also agreed to an extension with the Warriors that will keep him with the team through the 2026-27 season, ESPN said.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Big Game Bound: Pros detail offensive matchup battle in Super Bowl LIX
item.description: When it comes to offense, both the Kansas City Chiefs and Philadelphia Eagles are firing on all cylinders heading into Super Bowl LIX.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to sign order banning trans athletes from women's sports
item.description: The executive order will ban people biologically assigned males at birth from competing in women's sports.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Mahomes reveals thoughts on Trump attending the Super Bowl
item.description: Kansas City Chiefs quarterback Patrick Mahomes was asked what it will feel like to play in front of a sitting president.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Petrulis, NewsNation

item.title: 70 Illinois residents can win Las Vegas vacation with Rosati's Pizza
item.description: Illinois residents can enter the Rosati's Pizza sweepstakes and win a three-day, two-night vacation at the Circa Resort and Casino in Las Vegas, with the contest closing on the day of the Big Game.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Influencers, retired Bay Area sports stars headline NBA All-Star Celebrity Game in Oakland
item.description: (KRON) -- The NBA released the list of celebrities that will take the floor in Oakland for the NBA All-Star Celebrity Game on Feb. 14. A combination of influencers, retired athletes, film stars and more will put their basketball skills to test.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Jazz lose heartbreaker to Indiana, 112-111
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Jazz could not hold a 12-point lead in the final six minutes, and lose to the Indiana Pacers Monday night at the Delta Center, 112-111.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
